Coat a 96-well plate with vitronectin and block. Mix c(RGDfK) with human αvβ3 integrin, add to wells and incubate. Sequentially add anti-human αvβ3 primary antibody and peroxidase-labeled secondary antibody with washing steps. Finally, add chromogen for reaction, stop with sulfuric acid, and measure absorbance at 450 nm. Determine IC50 via inhibition curve to assess affinity, with quadruplicate tests.

Coat 96-well ELISA plates with vitronectin. Block non-specific binding sites with BSA-containing buffer. Add a fixed concentration of αvβ3 integrin and a series of diluted test inhibitors to each well. Wash away unbound components. Add mouse anti-human CD51/61 antibody, POD-conjugated anti-mouse IgG, and substrate. Stop the reaction and measure absorbance at 450 nm.

Vitronectin was biotinylated and dialyzed into PBS (pH 7.4). αvβ3 integrin was coated onto a 96-well plate (4°C, 1 h), blocked with 3% BSA (4°C, 1 h), and incubated with 14 nM biotinylated vitronectin plus diluted peptide (37°C, 3 h). After washing, ExtrAvidin-alkaline phosphatase (1:35000, RT, 1 h) was added. Color was developed using pNPP, and IC50 was calculated via nonlinear regression.

Prepare PRP from aspirin-free human donor blood, adjust platelet concentration to 300,000/µL. For each test, incubate 400µL PRP under stirring at 37°C, add 10µL solution containing varying concentrations of bitistatin (or its derivatives) for 1min. Then induce aggregation with ADP (final concentration 10µM), record light transmission changes over 5min. Calculate IC50 via least squares fitting to evaluate affinity for αᵢᵢbβ₃.

Seed HT-1080-FAP cells (tumor cells transfected with human FAP gene) into a 96-well plate to provide FAP targets for the binding reaction.Prepare the radiolabeled antibody [¹⁷⁷Lu]-DOTA-NCS-PKU525, and simultaneously prepare unlabeled antibody derivatives at different concentration gradients (0.01–200 nM), including DOTA-NCS-PKU525, DOTA-NHS-PKU525, and DOTA-MAL-PKU525.Add an equal volume of radiolabeled antibody to each well, and simultaneously add different concentrations of unlabeled antibody derivatives respectively. Incubate in a 37℃, 5% CO₂ incubator for 2 hours to allow the labeled and unlabeled antibodies to compete for binding to FAP on the cell surface.After incubation, remove the medium, wash the cells twice with PBS to remove unbound antibodies, harvest the cells, and determine the radioactivity intensity bound to the cells using a γ-counter.Plot a competition curve with the concentration of unlabeled antibody as the abscissa and the relative radioactivity intensity bound to the cells as the ordinate. Calculate the IC50 value (half-maximal inhibitory concentration) through curve fitting to indirectly characterize the binding affinity of the antibody. A lower IC50 value indicates higher affinity.

Incubate in a 37℃, 5% CO₂ incubator for 2 hours to allow the labeled and unlabeled antibodies to compete for binding to FAP on the cell surface.After incubation, remove the medium, wash the cells twice with PBS to remove unbound antibodies, harvest the cells, and determine the radioactivity intensity bound to the cells using a γ-counter.Plot a competition curve with the concentration of unlabeled antibody as the abscissa and the relative radioactivity intensity bound to the cells as the ordinate. Calculate the IC50 value (half-maximal inhibitory concentration) through curve fitting to indirectly characterize the binding affinity of the antibody. A lower IC50 value indicates higher affinity.

The binding interactions between the peptide ligands and recombinant GPC3 protein were assessed using MST with the Monolith NT.115 system (NanoTemper Technologies, Germany) and corresponding premium capillaries and reagents.42,43 Recombinant human GPC3 protein (Huazhi Tiancheng, Lanzhou, China) was used at a final concentration of 60 nM. To avoid potential artifacts caused by the metal chelate, the ligands tested were precursor peptides without radiometal complexation. Peptides were initially dissolved in DMSO, and to minimize signal fluctuations associated with excessively high ligand concentrations, the starting peptide concentration in the MST assay was set at 10 μM. From this concentration, serial two−fold dilutions were prepared down to 0.000305 μM.

For affinity determination of GGB02-F9 antibody via ELISA: First, dilute SEZ6 antigen and add it to microplate wells, incubate overnight at 4°C to immobilize the antigen. Discard unbound antigen, add buffer containing blocking agent (e.g., bovine serum albumin), and incubate at room temperature for 1-2 h to block non-specific binding sites on the carrier. Then, add serially diluted GGB02-F9 antibody solution, incubate at room temperature for 1-2 h to enable specific binding between the antibody and immobilized antigen. Wash the microplate repeatedly with PBS buffer containing Tween-20 to remove unbound free antibody. Next, add enzyme-labeled anti-human IgG secondary antibody (e.g., horseradish peroxidase-labeled) and incubate at room temperature for 30 min to 1 h for specific binding to GGB02-F9 bound to the antigen. Add substrate solution (e.g., TMB), incubate in the dark, and then add stop solution (e.g., sulfuric acid solution) to terminate the reaction. Finally, measure the absorbance (OD value) of each well at a specific wavelength (e.g., 450 nm) using a microplate reader.

PI3K dysregulation drives tumorigenesis, but PI3K inhibitors have severe off-tumor toxicity. This study designed [⁶⁸Ga]Ga/[¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-P1-4 based on IMM-H012. [¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-P4 showed superior pharmacodynamic/pharmacokinetic profiles, suppressed subcutaneous melanoma growth safely, and synergized with IMM-H012 via downregulating fatty acid oxidase. It revitalizes PI3K inhibitors as a promising theranostic for multiple cancers.

A lower IC50 indicates stronger binding affinity of the ligand for Nectin-4

Isothermal Titration Calorimetry (ITC) was employed. Annealed oligonucleotides were mixed with the peptide probe in a buffer containing 25 mM KH₂PO₄ and 60 mM KCl (pH 7.4), and the dissociation constant (K_D) values were obtained through thermodynamic parameter fitting.

This study engineered anti-mesothelin (MSLN) 2A10-VH-Fc fusion proteins with GRLR (G236R/L328R) and LALAPG (L234A/L235A/P329G) Fc mutations to abrogate FcγR binding, addressing off-target accumulation in Fc-rich organs of wild-type (WT) VH-Fc. Purified mutants (>95% purity) retained high MSLN affinity (K_D 2.2–3.7 nM) and silenced FcγR binding. After ⁸⁹Zr radiolabeling, PET imaging and biodistribution were tested in multiple MSLN-positive xenograft mouse models (HCT116, A431-G9/H9, AsPC-1). [⁸⁹Zr]Zr-2A10-VH-Fc_LALAPG showed drastically higher tumor uptake (13.0±0.1%ID/g at 120 h p.i. in HCT116) vs WT (4.2±0.6%ID/g), and markedly reduced liver/spleen accumulation. Both mutants exhibited sex-specific renal retention (females > males), unrelated to stability. LALAPG outperformed GRLR in in vivo stability and intratumoral distribution. These findings validate Fc-engineered VH-Fc, especially LALAPG, as promising PET agents for MSLN-expressing tumors, highlighting sex consideration in preclinical research.

The binding affinities of TPP-1 and NOTA-Asp₂-TPP-1 to human PD-L1 were quantified via Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR). This technique measured the dissociation constant (K_D), a key affinity index. TPP-1 had a K_D of 63.2 nM and NOTA-Asp₂-TPP-1 59.4 nM, both showing high PD-L1 binding affinity, with the Asp₂ modification not impairing the binding capacity.

This study develops 177Lu/68Ga-labeled EphA2-targeting cyclic peptide CEMJ4 (high affinity, good stability). Combined with HPK1 inhibitor, it shows synergistic anti-tumor effects in B16F10 models by inhibiting tumor growth, reversing 177Lu-induced T cell suppression, and increasing tumor-infiltrating immune cells, highlighting potential for refractory cold tumor theranostics.
